# Install `notion-database` â€” Notion Database

> Drop-in Notion-style database surface.

đź“š Knowledge base : https://resource.rahmanef.com/llms.txt
đź“¦ Slice detail   : https://resource.rahmanef.com/slices/notion-database
đź§  JSON catalog   : https://resource.rahmanef.com/api/knowledge?slice=notion-database
đź¤– Prompt page    : https://resource.rahmanef.com/agents/notion-database

## 1. Install

```bash
npx rahman-resources add notion-database
# alias: npx rr add notion-database
```

## 2. What it ships

- tags: `ui`, `notion`, `notion-like`, `database`, `table`, `board`, `list`, `gallery`, `calendar`, `feed`, `chart`, `dashboard`, `form`, `map`, `timeline`, `gantt`, `kanban`, `views`, `filter`, `sort`, `property`, `files`, `person`, `formula`, `timestamp`, `unique-id`, `csv`, `json`, `import`, `export`, `template`, `data`, `backup`, `primitive`, `optional`, `embeddable`

## 3. Wire it up

**Controlled component.** `<NotionDatabase />` renders the whole surface â€” 11 views (table, board, list, gallery, calendar, feed, chart, dashboard, form, map, timeline), 18 cell types, filter / sort / group / calculate, row peek + multi-select, table cell drag-fill, CSV / JSON import-export. It is 100% props-driven: it owns NO data state â€” you hold `db` + `rows` and persist every change callback. The view tab strip scrolls horizontally and the card clips to its border, so it stays inside any container width.

**1. Install** â€” `npx rr add notion-database`. Cascades the `notion-shell` peer (the domain types live there). Components import from `@/features/notion-database`; types from `@/features/notion-shell`.

**2. Minimal wire-up** â€” keep `db: Database` + `rows: Page[]` in your store (a Convex query result or `useState`) and pass change handlers:
```tsx
import { NotionDatabase } from '@/features/notion-database';

<NotionDatabase
  db={db}
  rows={rows}
  onRowAdd={addRow}
  onRowUpdate={(rowId, propId, value) => setValue(rowId, propId, value)}
  onRowRemove={removeRow}
  onPropertyAdd={addProperty}
  onViewActivate={setActiveView}
  onViewAdd={addView}
  onViewConfigChange={(viewId, patch) => patchView(viewId, patch)}
/>
```
Omit any callback and that affordance goes read-only; pass `readOnly` to freeze everything at once.

**3. Data shape** â€” `Database = { id, name, properties: Property[], views: DatabaseViewConfig[], activeViewId }`; each row `Page = { id, title, rowProps: Record<propId, PropertyValue> }`. For `relation` / `rollup` cells also pass `pages` + `databases`; for `person` / `created_by` cells pass `userLookup(id)`.

**4. Import / export** â€” mount `<DatabaseIOActions db={db} rows={rows} onImport={handleImport} />` in your toolbar: CSV/JSON in (with schema-diff), CSV/JSON + live-schema templates out. New columns arrive with a `tempId` â€” map it to your real backend id before writing their `rowProps`.

**5. Backend (optional)** â€” the UI is store-agnostic. For Convex persistence copy `template-base/database-silong/convex/` (handlers â†’ `convex/`, schema fragment merges into `convex/schema.ts`). Pick `_shared/minimal/` (single-user, noop authz) or `_shared/full/` (`@convex-dev/auth` + workspaces). See CONVEX-BACKEND.md.

**Just one view?** Import it directly â€” `import { TableView } from '@/features/notion-database'` â€” and feed it `rows` + `renderCell` + `renderColumnHeader`.

## Tools (agentic surface)

Function-calling tools this slice provides. Register the exported collection on any `@/shared/agentic` host (e.g. `useAgentTools(<x>Tools, ctx)`) and ONE agent can drive this slice alongside others.

**Agent guidance:** Notion-style database. rows to read the current view; row.delete is irreversible; view.switch/filter/sort change presentation only.

- `notion-database.rows`
- `notion-database.row.add`
- `notion-database.row.update`
- `notion-database.row.delete` âš  destructive
- `notion-database.view.switch`
- `notion-database.filter`
- `notion-database.sort`

âš  destructive tools are flagged `dangerous: true` â€” wire the agent loop's `confirm` event so they need user approval before running.

rr ships the function list above plus a custom instruction (`registry.systemPrompt()`); bring your own model + key to call them (BYOK).

## Rules of engagement

- shadcn-only UI primitives. No raw `<button>` / `<dialog>` / native date or file inputs.
- 200-line hard cap per source file (extract neighbours when over).
- All Convex queries hit an index (`.withIndex(...)`); never bare `.collect()`.
- Public mutations/queries declare `args:` validators + authz.
- Full ruleset: https://resource.rahmanef.com/best-practice
